article_items.title: How Rippling built production AI in 6 months with Deep Agents and LangSmith
article_items.description: Rippling uses LangChain Deep Agents and LangSmith to run cross-domain AI across HR, IT, finance, payroll, and global operations.
article_items.excerpt: Rippling uses LangChain Deep Agents and LangSmith to run cross-domain AI across HR, IT, finance, payroll, and global operations.
article_items.summary: Rippling AI: Built On Deep Agents + LangSmith The speed by which Rippling integrated AI agents was possible because the team built with LangChain from the start, using composable agent primitives and a shared observability layer in LangSmith. When Deep Agents launched, they adopted it for Rippling AI's core reasoning loop. The team shipped it in roughly 6 months. For teams building AI on complex, permission-sensitive platforms, the Rippling team's advice is direct: Deep Agents is the reasoning framework behind Rippling AI. Rather than asking the LLM to manipulate data directly, Rippling’s action agents use sandboxed code execution to normalize inputs (say, a CSV from a client) into the format Rippling's internal tools expect. Three types of specialized Deep Agents sit beneath a supervisor agent: Read agents query structured data across.

article_items.description: Nvidia Taps Unitree for Humanoid Robot Platform

article_items.summary: Unitree said the H2 Plus humanoid robot is set for release in late 2026, while Nvidia said GR00T workflows for the Unitree G1 will be available soon on GitHub and Hugging Face. Nvidia on Monday said it has selected Chinese robotics company Unitree as the hardware partner for its new robotics system for researchers -- the first of its kind sold by the chipmaker. "The Nvidia Isaac GR00T Reference Humanoid Robot built on H2 Plus gives researchers a single, open platform to make breakthrough discoveries toward general-purpose physical intelligence." According to Nvidia, the robot includes 75 degrees of freedom across its body and hands, stereo vision sensors, wrist-mounted cameras, and onboard compute capable of real-time sensor processing and robot inference. Nvidia on Monday said it has selected Chinese robotics company Unitree as the hardware partner for its new robotics system for researchers -- the first of its kind sold by the chipmaker.

"The Nvidia Isaac GR00T Reference Humanoid Robot built on H2 Plus gives researchers a single, open platform to make breakthrough discoveries toward general-purpose physical intelligence." According to Nvidia, the robot.
The AI chipmaker said it will combine its Jetson Thor onboard computing system and Isaac GR00T humanoid AI models with Unitree’s humanoid robot, the H2 Plus , for the platform.

Nvidia said the platform is aimed at research institutions and robotics developers, including universities such as Stanford and ETH Zurich, providing a standardized foundation for developing humanoid capabilities.
By integrating hardware, simulation and AI software into a single design , the companies said they can help researchers accelerate humanoid development and real-world testing.
"Humanoid robots will bring physical AI to the world's largest industries, opening a multitrillion-dollar economic opportunity," Huang said in a release .

Nvidia Taps Unitree for Humanoid Robot Platform Nvidia Taps Unitree for Humanoid Robot Platform Nvidia is combining Unitree’s humanoid hardware with its own AI and simulation tools, in a new design aimed at researchers.
Nvidia’s Isaac GR00T developer platform will also be used to support Unitree’s G1 humanoid robot.
Scarlett also has a background in the mining and resources sector, with experience at Mine Australia, Mine Technology and Power Technology.
Scarlett Evans is a freelance writer with a focus on emerging technologies and the minerals industry.
Previously, she served as assistant editor at IoT World Today, where she specialized in robotics and smart city technologies.

rag_rules: Make the business move concrete: who is involved, what changed, and why now.
